condition_specific: heart disease, lung disease, respiratory tract condition
notes: Covers paid firefighters only (2,080 hrs/year). A firefighter who received a service-connected disability for 6+ months due to violence or illness other than heart/lung/respiratory, then returned to service, cannot invoke the presumption for heart/lung/respiratory for 2 years after return unless clear and convincing evidence of direct causation from firefighter service.

condition_specific: heart disease, lung disease, respiratory tract condition
notes: Same statute and same conditions as cardiovascular row; lung disease and respiratory tract condition covered alongside heart disease. Paid firefighters only (2,080 hrs/year). 2-year re-entry exclusion applies (see cardiovascular row).

filing_deadline_notes: Cause of action arises on the date (after May 12, 2015) that the employee: suffers disability, knows or should have known the disease is caused by employment, and files a claim.
notes: Covers paid and volunteer firefighters (34A-3-113 references 67-20-2(5)(b)(ii) which includes volunteers). Annual physical examinations required during employment. Must have regularly responded to firefighting or emergency calls within the 8-year period. Tobacco exclusion: no tobacco use for 8 years preceding report to employer. If employed by multiple employers, last-substantial-exposure employer/insurer is liable. Limited cancer list: pharynx, esophagus, lung, mesothelioma only.

filing_deadline_notes: Cause of action arises on the date (after May 12, 2015) that the employee: suffers disability, knows or should have known the disease is caused by employment, and files a claim.
notes: Statute defines firefighter to include volunteer members per 67-20-2(5)(b)(ii). Same 8-year service and annual exam requirements apply. Tobacco exclusion: no tobacco use for 8 years preceding report.

condition_specific: diseases defined under Section 78B-8-401 (bloodborne/communicable diseases) contracted through significant exposure
filing_deadline_notes: Must test positive during tenure of employment or within 3 months after termination.
notes: Two pathways: (1) Employment commenced before July 1, 1988: tests positive during employment or within 3 months post-termination; (2) Employment commenced on/after July 1, 1988: must test negative at commencement AND 3 months later, then test positive during employment or within 3 months post-termination. Agency must inform providers of provisions at commencement and termination.
